page.title: Settings
page.subtitle: Manage workspace preferences, models, capability sources, and local data

descriptions.models: Configure AI providers, model lists, default models, and automatic routing for AgentBoard.
descriptions.agents: Manage reusable agent roles, commands, and collaboration behavior.
descriptions.automations: Manage schedules, cron work, run history, and replay behavior.
descriptions.security: Control tool auto-approval, browser capabilities, and high-risk permissions.
descriptions.system: Configure proxy, developer options, and app-level runtime preferences.

general.title: General Settings
general.subtitle: Theme, language & basic preferences

themePreset.title: Theme Preset
themePreset.desc: Choose a built-in palette. It applies globally across the app, SSH workspaces, and terminals.

mulberry.label: AgentBoard Workbench
mulberry.desc: Graphite, paper, sage, and amber accents tuned for long knowledge, task, and code sessions.

studio.label: Studio Blue
studio.desc: A calm blue-gray workspace tuned for coding and host management.

graphite.label: Graphite
graphite.desc: A restrained neutral system with stronger contrast and a more editorial feel.

ocean.label: Ocean
ocean.desc: A deeper teal-blue palette that gives terminals and status panes more depth.

forest.label: Forest
forest.desc: A softer green system that stays easy on the eyes during long terminal sessions.

dawn.label: Dawn
dawn.desc: A warm copper palette with more glow and texture in light mode.

appearance.title: Appearance
appearance.subtitle: Background and typography preferences
appearance.backgroundDesc: Override only the app background; leave empty to follow theme
appearance.fontDesc: Select a font preset for the entire app

general.animationsDesc: Enabled by default. Turning this off disables transitions, motion effects, and smooth scrolling as much as possible.

liveOutputAnimation.title: AI live output animation
liveOutputAnimation.desc: Choose the motion style used while the assistant is streaming a reply.

agile.label: Agile
agile.desc: Faster, sharper pulses for a more responsive feel.

elegant.label: Elegant
elegant.desc: Softer, slower motion with a calmer rhythm.

general.systemProxyDesc: Set the proxy URL used by the whole app process, for example http://127.0.0.1:7890 or http://host:port.
general.toolbarCollapsedByDefaultDesc: Off by default. Initializes the left toolbar state when the app opens.
general.teamToolsDesc: Enable Agent Team collaboration, allowing AI to create and manage parallel teams
general.teamToolsEnabled: Enabled: AI can use TeamCreate, Task(run_in_background) and other tools for multi-agent collaboration
general.builtinBrowserDesc: Enable the built-in browser panel and browser automation tools, allowing the agent to navigate websites, extract content, take screenshots, click elements, and fill forms
general.builtinBrowserEnabled: Enabled: Agent can use BrowserNavigate, BrowserGetContent, BrowserScreenshot, BrowserSnapshot, BrowserClick, BrowserType, BrowserScroll
general.maxParallelToolCalls: Max parallel tool calls per turn
general.maxParallelToolCallsDesc: Control how many independent tool calls from the same model turn can run at once. Higher values can speed up Bash-heavy workflows, but increase local load.
general.clarifyAutoAcceptRecommendedDesc: Off by default. Only applies in Clarify mode; when enabled, inline prompt recommendations start an 8-second countdown and automatically accept the current suggestion.
general.autoApproveDesc: Skip permission confirmation for all tool calls

update.downloadedRestarting: Update v{{version}} downloaded. Restarting to install…

projectDefaultDirectory.title: Default project directory
projectDefaultDirectory.desc: When creating a project, entering a project name creates a same-named folder here. If custom mode is off, the last used location is preferred.
projectDefaultDirectory.useCustomDesc: When disabled, prefer the directory from the last created project.

general.autoApproveWarning: Dangerous: All tool calls will execute automatically without confirmation
general.devModeDesc: Show request debug info in AI responses when errors occur
general.contextCompressionDesc: When conversation approaches context window limit, automatically compress history messages and preserve core information
general.contextCompressionEnabled: Enabled: Auto-compress when context usage exceeds 90%, original task and recent messages will be fully preserved
general.editorWorkspaceDesc: Use the current session or project working folder as the preferred URI context for code editors
general.editorWorkspaceEnabled: Enabled: Local files bind to the current working folder and can reuse lightweight cross-file semantics
general.editorRemoteLanguageService: Enable remote language service in SSH sessions
general.editorRemoteLanguageServiceDesc: Experimental: Allow SSH files to use remote workspace URIs and reserve context for future advanced language-service bridging
general.editorRemoteLanguageServiceEnabled: Enabled: SSH files use ssh:// URIs as editor model paths
general.toolResultFormatDesc: Choose whether structured tool results default to TOON or JSON. Only affects future tool calls; existing results stay unchanged.
general.resetConfirm: Reset all settings to default? API Keys will be preserved.

data.title: Data Management
data.subtitle: Backup, import or clear conversation data
data.backupDesc: Export all conversations as a JSON backup file.
data.importDesc: Restore sessions from a JSON backup file.
data.clearDesc: Delete every session and message. This cannot be undone.
data.clearConfirm: Delete all {{count}} sessions? This cannot be undone.

memory.title: Global Profile & Memory
memory.subtitle: Manage PROFILE.md, FOCUS.md, MEMORY.md, and daily memory files under ~/.agentboard
memory.effectiveHint: These files reload on the next agent request; main and shared sessions load different layers.
memory.missingFileHint: {{file}} does not exist yet. A starter template is loaded; click Save to create it.
memory.loadFailed: Failed to load global identity/memory files: {{error}}

tabDescriptions.profile: Captures durable collaboration preferences, product taste, and delivery habits.
tabDescriptions.focus: Tracks current cross-project focus, near-term tasks, and deferred scope.
tabDescriptions.memory: Stores cross-project long-term memory and durable decisions.
tabDescriptions.daily: Keeps short-term notes for today that can later be distilled into MEMORY.md.

analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.subtitle: Inspect request-level usage by time, model, and provider
analytics.clearConfirmDescription: Permanently delete {{count}} log entries created before {{date}}. This cannot be undone.
analytics.clearEmpty: No logs older than 7 days to clear

model.title: Model Configuration
model.subtitle: Select AI provider and model, adjust generation parameters
model.noProvidersHint: Please go to the "AI Providers" page to enable a provider and finish auth setup
model.mainModelDesc: Used for main chat requests and Agent loops; can be Auto or a fixed manual model
model.autoModeDesc: Auto first uses the fast model to classify the latest input by task type, then routes to the main or fast model. Lightweight rewrites, summaries, translations, and formatting are more likely to hit the fast model. Falls back to the main model on failure.
model.manualModeDesc: Manual mode always uses the selected main model below.
model.manualMainCandidateDesc: This model is used both as the fixed manual model and as the main branch target when Auto routes to main.
model.noModelsHint: No models available for this provider, please go to "AI Providers" to add models
model.fastModelDesc: For Auto routing classification, lightweight main-chat requests, title generation, and sub-agent tasks (cheaper, faster)
model.promptRecommendationDesc: Used for inline ghost suggestions and Tab acceptance. Configure per mode.

promptRecommendationModesDesc.chat: For general conversation and next-question suggestions
promptRecommendationModesDesc.clarify: For requirement clarification and follow-up suggestions
promptRecommendationModesDesc.agent: For agent-driven execution and task breakdown suggestions
promptRecommendationModesDesc.code: For implementation, modification, and debugging suggestions
promptRecommendationModesDesc.acp: For goal clarification, architecture design, and delegation suggestions in ACP control flows

model.disableRecommendation: Not configured (disable recommendations for this mode)
model.noModelsAvailable: No models available for this provider
model.translationModelDesc: Default model used for the AI Translate page
model.imageModelDesc: Default global image model used by the image plugin
model.noImageModels: No enabled image models found. Enable one in AI Providers first.

newSessionDefaultModel.title: Default model for new sessions
newSessionDefaultModel.desc: Control which model new sessions bind to by default. If you follow the current active model, the top-bar selection is reused.

model.temperatureDesc: Control the randomness of generated text

plugin.title: Local Tools
plugin.subtitle: Configure built-in browser, image, and desktop tool access rules
plugin.enableDesc: The Agent only gets the related Tool after the plugin is enabled and configured
plugin.modelSourceDesc: Reuse the global image model or override it for this plugin
plugin.globalModelMissing: No global image model is configured yet. Set one in Model Configuration first.
plugin.toolStatusDesc: The image Tool is only exposed when the plugin is enabled and the model is valid.
plugin.toolStatusDescBrowser: Browser tools are only exposed when the browser plugin is enabled. Domain rules apply to manual browsing and Agent navigation.
plugin.toolStatusDescDesktop: Desktop-control tools are only exposed when the plugin is enabled. Every call still requires approval. If the active model also enables Computer Use, OpenAI Computer Use will proxy these capabilities first.

toolArgsMap.BrowserType: Available args: selector, text, clear, submit
toolArgsMap.DesktopClick: Available args: x, y, button, action
toolArgsMap.DesktopScroll: Available args: x, y, scrollX, scrollY

status.disabled: The plugin is disabled, so its tools are not exposed to the Agent.
status.not_ready: The plugin is enabled, but required configuration is still missing.
status.ready: The plugin is ready and its tools are available to the Agent.

browser.title: Browser privacy and access
browser.desc: Clear browser cookies and configure which domains the built-in browser can visit.
browser.blockedDomainsDesc: Navigation to these domains and subdomains is denied.
browser.allowedDomainsDesc: Leave empty to allow all domains. When set, only matching domains can be opened.
browser.domainRuleHint: Enter one domain per line or separate with commas. Blocked domains take priority over allowed domains; subdomains match automatically.

image.title: Image Plugin
image.description: Expose a dedicated image-generation Tool to the Agent.
image.promptDesc: Enable this only when you want the Agent to directly generate images. The Tool accepts only `prompt` and `count`, and uses the configured image model.

browser.title: Browser Plugin
browser.description: Expose the built-in browser panel and browser automation tools to the Agent.
browser.promptDesc: The Agent can navigate pages, extract content, take screenshots, inspect elements, click, type, and scroll. Domain allow/block rules are enforced before navigation.

desktop-control.title: Desktop Control Plugin
desktop-control.description: Expose desktop screenshot, click, scroll, and keyboard tools to the Agent.
desktop-control.promptDesc: These tools can inspect and control the full desktop. Keep approval enabled, capture the screen before acting, and use them only when direct desktop automation is required. If the active model enables Computer Use, OpenAI Computer Use will coordinate these desktop capabilities first.

migration.title: Import from Other Clients
migration.subtitle: Bring providers, commands, agents, MCP servers, and instructions into AgentBoard
migration.sourceDesc: Currently reads only ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json and migrates providers, model selections, commands, agents, MCP servers, and instructions.
migration.backupHint: Back up target files before apply
migration.empty: No migration items yet, or preview has not been generated.
migration.resultSubtitle: Result of the latest apply operation.
migration.backupPathUnavailable: No backup created for this run (no existing target files required backup)
migration.applyPartial: Migration finished with {{failed}} failure(s); {{applied}} item(s) applied.

about.title: About
about.subtitle: Version info & project links
about.description: AgentBoard is a personal AI workbench powered by a local runtime and desktop tools.
about.heroDescription: Coordinate multiple models, agents, and skills inside one native workspace.
about.workflowDescription: Ship code, manage files, run shell, and orchestrate MCP tools without losing context.

orchestration.title: Multi-agent orchestration
orchestration.desc: Run coordinated agent teams to decompose complex work.

sandbox.title: Local secure sandbox
sandbox.desc: Keep every file and secret local with auditable execution.

channels.title: Connectors & Skills
channels.desc: Extend the workbench with Connectors, Skill Sources, and local Markdown resources.

about.summary: AgentBoard unifies multi-model workflows, connectors, skills, local Agents, and Commands inside one desktop workbench while keeping policy, context, memory, and task state under a local runtime boundary.

dialog.title: Quick Settings
dialog.subtitle: Fast model switching and parameter tuning
dialog.fastModelDesc: For Auto routing classification, lightweight main-chat requests, title generation, and SubAgent tasks

accounts.title: OAuth Accounts
accounts.empty: No accounts yet. Add one to enable multi-account failover.

confirmRemove.title: Remove account
confirmRemove.description: Remove {{email}} from this provider? Tokens will be revoked locally.

import.title: Import accounts from JSON
import.description: Paste an array of OAuth records. Each record must include an email field.

toasts.fileApiMissing: File picker API is not available

provider.title: AI Providers
provider.subtitle: Configure API keys and models for each provider
provider.contextCompressionThresholdDesc: Trigger full compression at this context usage ratio. Range: {{min}}% - {{max}}%
provider.noModelsHint: Fetch from API or add manually
provider.deleteProviderConfirm: Delete provider "{{name}}"? This cannot be undone.
provider.addCustomProviderDesc: Add a custom AI provider. It defaults to OpenAI-compatible and can be switched to Anthropic, Responses, or Gemini.
provider.protocolAutoHint: Built-in providers use the matching protocol automatically; usually you only need to add credentials.
provider.protocolCustomHint: Custom providers default to OpenAI-compatible. Switch only when your provider requires it.
provider.baseUrlHint: Base URL for the API endpoint
provider.proxyUrlHint: Built-in providers use the official default endpoint automatically; edit only for proxies or compatible services.
provider.modelManagementDesc: Manage global model defaults that provider copies inherit from
provider.modelManagementHint: Provider-side models inherit defaults from here first; later edits under a provider only affect that local copy.
provider.modelManagementDeleteConfirm: Delete global model "{{name}}"? It will not be auto-added again later.
provider.thinkConfigDesc: Configure deep thinking parameters for model {{model}}
provider.thinkBodyParamsHint: Extra parameters merged into request body when Think is enabled
provider.thinkDisabledBodyParamsHint: Parameters merged into request body when Think is disabled, leave empty to skip
provider.reasoningEffortLevelsHint: When selected, the chat bar shows effort choices; when empty, it only shows a Think toggle
provider.defaultReasoningEffortHint: The level used the first time Think is enabled
provider.forceTemperatureHint: Anthropic requires temperature=1, leave empty to not override
provider.thinkJsonObjError: Parameters must be a JSON object
provider.addModelDesc: Manually add a model and configure its parameters
provider.modelTypeOverrideHint: Leave unset to use the provider default (current: {{type}})
provider.modelTypeOverrideGlobalHint: Optional. Leave empty to let each provider copy keep using its own default protocol.
provider.modelCategoryHint: Tag how this model is used (chat, speech, embedding, image)
provider.oauthRequired: Please sign in with OAuth first
provider.oauthManualDesc: Paste JSON containing access_token and refresh_token (optional expires_at / expires_in). Stored locally.
provider.channelAppTokenOptional: Public apps do not require an App Token

channel.title: channles
channel.subtitle: Configure and manage your channles
channel.addChannelDesc: Choose a channle provider to add.
channel.addPluginDesc: Choose a channle provider to add.
channel.modelHint: Model used for channle auto-reply. Leave default to use the globally active model.
channel.autoReplyDesc: Automatically reply to incoming messages using the Agent
channel.streamingReplyDesc: Stream responses in real-time via CardKit (Feishu only)
channel.autoStartDesc: Automatically start this channle when the app launches
channel.conversationsDesc: Channle conversation history will appear here.
channel.allowReadHomeDesc: Allow reading files under your home directory (~)
channel.readablePathsDesc: Whitelist specific directories the channle can read
channel.allowWriteOutsideDesc: Allow writing files outside the channle directory
channel.allowSubAgentsDesc: Allow using Task and other sub-agent tools

weixin.bindingDesc: Bind by QR code, then use long polling for inbound and outbound messages.
weixin.baseUrlMissing: Please fill in the Weixin gateway URL first
weixin.scanHint: Please scan and confirm in Weixin

toolsDesc.PluginSendMessage: Send a message via the channle
toolsDesc.PluginReplyMessage: Reply to a specific message via the channle
toolsDesc.PluginGetGroupMessages: Read recent messages from a chat/group
toolsDesc.PluginGetCurrentChatMessages: Read recent messages from the current channle chat
toolsDesc.FeishuSendImage: Send an image to a Feishu chat
toolsDesc.FeishuSendFile: Send a file to a Feishu chat
toolsDesc.FeishuListChatMembers: List members in a Feishu chat (for @mentions)
toolsDesc.FeishuAtMember: Mention members in a Feishu group chat
toolsDesc.FeishuSendUrgent: Send an urgent push (app/sms) for a message
toolsDesc.FeishuBitableListTables: List tables in a Bitable app
toolsDesc.FeishuBitableListFields: List fields in a Bitable table
toolsDesc.FeishuBitableGetRecords: Read records from a Bitable table
toolsDesc.FeishuBitableCreateRecords: Create records in a Bitable table
toolsDesc.FeishuBitableUpdateRecords: Update records in a Bitable table
toolsDesc.FeishuBitableDeleteRecords: Delete records from a Bitable table

mcp.title: Connections
mcp.subtitle: Connect MCP servers and third-party tool providers
mcp.addServerDesc: Add a local or remote MCP-compatible connector.
mcp.importJsonHint: Paste either `{ "mcpServers": { ... } }` or the server object directly. Each key is imported as a server name.
mcp.importJsonInvalid: No valid MCP server config found
mcp.name: Name
mcp.autoFallbackDesc: Fall back to SSE if Streamable HTTP fails

skillsmarket.title: Skill Sources
skillsmarket.subtitle: Configure built-in skills, external directories, GitHub imports, and connector-backed sources
skillsmarket.sourceDesc: Used by the Skills market list. If an external site API is unstable, URL and local folder import still work.
skillsmarket.sourceConfigDesc: Switch the market source, or open external directories and import after review.
skillsmarket.providerDesc: Select where to fetch skills from
skillsmarket.skillsmpDesc: External skill sources (API key optional per source)
skillsmarket.builtinDesc: Local bundled skills list (no API key required)
skillsmarket.builtinInfo: Bundled skills ship with AgentBoard, work offline, and are safe defaults for document, spreadsheet, OCR, PDF, web, and office workflows.
skillsmarket.curatedDesc: Packages from ClawHub, SkillHub, or GitHub all go through local preview and safety checks.
skillsmarket.externalDesc: ClawHub, GitHub URLs, and vendor catalogs should open in preview-only mode first. Install after reviewing files, scripts, and requested permissions.
skillsmarket.connectorDesc: Tencent ADP/Yuanqi, cloud plugins, and MCP servers belong under External Connectors because they run as service integrations rather than local skills.
skillsmarket.skillsmpInfo: Fetches skills from the configured external source. Public browsing should not need a key; private repositories, rate limits, and publishing can use source-specific credentials.
skillsmarket.apiKeyDesc: Optional: use only when the selected source needs a Bearer token, private repository access, or higher rate limits. Tencent service credentials belong in Connectors.
skillsmarket.apiKeyRequired: Public sources do not require an API key by default.
skillsmarket.testDesc: Refresh the current source skill list

builtin.title: Built-in Skills
builtin.desc: Bundled with the app, available offline, and best for stable document, spreadsheet, OCR, PDF, browser, and office automation workflows.

github.title: GitHub URL
github.desc: Public repositories do not need a key. Later imports can support raw files, zips, and release assets; private repositories can use a GitHub token.

skillhub.title: SkillHub
skillhub.desc: A Chinese directory source. Prefer official APIs; without a stable API, open the directory instead of crawling it by default.

clawhub.title: ClawHub / OpenClaw
clawhub.desc: The default external registry for searching, previewing, and installing public skill packages.

connectors.title: Vendor Connectors
connectors.desc: Tencent ADP/Yuanqi, cloud plugins, and MCP servers are service integrations. Keep their credentials in Connectors, not Skill Sources.

review.title: Install Review
review.desc: Remote skills download to a temporary folder first, show files, scripts, and risks, then install to ~/.agentboard/skills after approval.

keyPolicy.title: Key Policy
keyPolicy.builtin: Built-in skills: no key and no network access required.
keyPolicy.github: GitHub: public repositories need no key; private repositories or rate limits can use a GitHub token.
keyPolicy.external: SkillHub / ClawHub: no AgentBoard-owned key for public browsing. If a source requires auth, store a source-specific Bearer token.
keyPolicy.connectors: Tencent and cloud services: configure keys in Connectors or MCP, not in Skill Sources.

installPolicy.title: Install Policy
installPolicy.desc: Do not crawl third-party directories by default. Prefer official APIs or user-provided URLs. Every remote skill enters preview, scan, and security review before install.

websearch.title: Web Search
websearch.subtitle: Configure web search providers and settings
websearch.enableDesc: Allow the AI to search the web for current information
websearch.providerDesc: Select a search provider to use
websearch.apiKeyDesc: API key for the selected provider
websearch.baseUrlDesc: Custom base URL for the API (optional)
websearch.maxResultsDesc: Maximum number of search results to return
websearch.disabledError: Web search is disabled. Please enable it in settings.
websearch.apiKeyRequired: API key is required for this provider.
websearch.testSuccess: Web search test successful! Found {{count}} results.

shortcuts.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
shortcuts.subtitle: Quick actions available throughout the app
shortcuts.inputHistory: Cycle sent history with up/down (caret at start/end)
